articles.title: Report: Nearly a quarter of Israelis considering leaving if Netanyahu is re-elected
articles.summary: Report: Nearly a quarter of Israelis considering leaving if Netanyahu is re-elected About 23 percent of Israelis are considering leaving the country, or have a relative who is, if Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is re-elected, according to a Channel 12 poll reported by The Times of Israel. The survey found that another 68 percent said they were not considering leaving the country, and the remaining respondents were unsure. The poll also found that 67 percent opposed legislation passed by Netanyahu's coalition this week, including a law enshrining Torah study as a quasi-constitutional value a

articles.title: Iran strikes: Jordan intercepts 10 missiles, Bahrain sounds sirens
articles.summary: Iran strikes: Jordan intercepts 10 missiles, Bahrain sounds sirens The Jordanian military has said its air defences downed 10 Iranian missiles that breached the country’s airspace early on Saturday morning. The military said the interceptions caused no casualties or material damage, and Royal Engineers have begun clearing and securing debris from the impact sites. Iran’s IRNA posted on X “footage demonstrating Iranian missiles breaching Jordan's air defence networks and rendering the US Patriot systems entirely ineffective”. articles.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night

articles.title: Hormuz traffic falls to three-week low, MarineTraffic says
articles.summary: Hormuz traffic falls to three-week low, MarineTraffic says Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz fell to a three-week low of eight vessels on Thursday, down from 15 the previous day, ship-tracking platform MarineTraffic said. In a post on X, MarineTraffic said seven of the eight transits used the Iranian shipping lane, with no vessels crossing via the Omani route and no shadow fleet movements recorded. articles.summary: Centcom says US military completes seventh consecutive wave of strikes on Iran US Central Command (Centcom) said it struck Iranian surveillance sites, military logistics infrastructure, underground weapons storage facilities and maritime assets in its seventh consecutive wave of attacks overnight on 17-18 July. In a statement posted on X, Centcom said US forces used fighter aircraft, drones, warships and other military assets in the operation. articles.summary: EU asks Israel to refrain from expanding West Bank settlements The European Union has reiterated its call on Israel to refrain from expanding its settlements in the occupied West Bank. "The EU reiterates its call on Israel to refrain from further settlement expansion, the legalization of outposts, land appropriation, demolitions, evictions and other unilateral measures that undermine the viability of the two-state solution," said a statement from the EU's diplomatic service spokesperson. articles.title: Elders warn of potential disappearance of Palestinian territories
articles.summary: Elders warn of potential disappearance of Palestinian territories Four members of the Elders group of international figures founded by Nelson Mandela have warned of the potential disappearance of the Palestinian territories and called for an end to Israeli "impunity". They said in a statement that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government was seeking "to make Palestine disappear physically, economically, culturally and politically." articles.title: Report: Israeli settlers spend full day inside occupied Syrian territory
articles.summary: Report: Israeli settlers spend full day inside occupied Syrian territory A group of Israeli settlers illegally crossed the border into Syria and spent a full day there before being apprehended by Israeli troops on Friday, according to the Times of Israel. This was the latest of several recent incidents in which settlers have entered the Syrian territory occupied by Israel’s army. The settlers, who had crossed the border near Majdal Shams, were handed over to the Israeli police for questioning, the Israeli military said, according to the TOI.

articles.title: Iranian official calls US strike on Hormuzgan 'blatant war crime'
articles.summary: Iranian official calls US strike on Hormuzgan 'blatant war crime' Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei has termed the US strike on Hormuzgan province, in which eight civilians were killed, as a “blatant war crime”. In a post on X, Baqaei said the attacks targeted bridges and killed four men and four women, including two brothers with disabilities.  He added that the attacks had strengthened the resolve of the Iranian people, saying they were now "more determined and united than ever" and would make their "enemies bitterly regret this criminal aggression". articles.title: IRGC says Hormuz fully sealed off as it has become 'highly perilous'
articles.summary: IRGC says Hormuz fully sealed off as it has become 'highly perilous' Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy "unequivocally announces" that the Strait of Hormuz has become "highly perilous" and is now fully sealed off due to the "crimes of the US military", state broadcaster IRIB reported. The Navy added that until Washington ceases its "acts of aggression", "not a single shipment of chemical fertilizer nor even a drop of oil or gas will be allowed to leave the region". articles.title: IRIB: US missiles hit Larak Island maritime traffic control facility
articles.summary: IRIB: US missiles hit Larak Island maritime traffic control facility US missiles struck the central mast of the Ports and Maritime Organisation's maritime traffic control facility on Larak Island, according to Iran's state broadcaster IRIB. IRIB also reported that Iran's army said its Arash suicide drones targeted a US military ammunition depot at Camp Al-Udairi and ammunition storage facilities at Ali Al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait. The army said several bridges in Kuwait that the US uses for military logistics were targeted in response to US attacks on Iran's transport bridges.

The official added that eight other people were injured in the attacks, the report said. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, meanwhile, said that they “stopped” four ships trying to transit the Strait of Hormuz and claimed that two oil tankers exploded after hitting the mines in the waterway. articles.title: Saudi Arabia says danger passed in Al-Kharj, Yanbu after 'potential danger' warnings
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia says danger passed in Al-Kharj, Yanbu after 'potential danger' warnings The Saudi civil defence issued two early warnings for Al-Kharj city and Yanbu early Saturday to be alert to "potential danger," but it later said the danger had passed in both areas, without providing details on the danger that triggered the warnings, according to Reuters. Iran targeted an American military base in Saudi Arabia with a ballistic missile, the first time that the Islamic Republic has directly attacked the kingdom in four months, a US official had told Axios.

articles.title: Israeli strike on funeral kills eight, wounds 22 in Gaza
articles.summary: Israeli strike on funeral kills eight, wounds 22 in Gaza Gaza's civil defence agency and a hospital said eight people were killed in an Israeli airstrike on a gathering of civilians in the Al-Balata market area of Nuseirat refugee camp. The area's Al-Awda hospital said it received eight dead and 22 people wounded in the strike. Mahmoud Bassal, spokesman for the civil defence agency, told AFP the strike occurred during a funeral procession. articles.summary: IRGC says US drone downed over Bushehr as projectiles hit Yazd Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy said it intercepted and shot down a US MQ-9 drone over the southern port city of Bushehr using a newly developed air defence system. Separately, Iran's Mehr News Agency reported that projectiles fired by the US landed outside the central city of Yazd. No casualties were reported.
